standard_win: The plaque simply reads:"CEO office". This room, in stark contrast to the other rooms in that wing, is relatively somber and minimalistic.
It prioritizes functionality but the materials are top tier and there's a billiard table.
Maybe it looks a bit underwhelming compared to previous halls, but it looks like the perfect office.
full_win: If previous rooms weren't enough, you still weren't prepared for this.
The plaque reads:"Hall of the Great Council replica, Doge's Palace"
While the hall itself is a masterpiece, as majestic as you could imagine with the largest oil painting on canvas in the world, what's inside is truly beyond belief.
This hall is used as an assembly line for microprocessors and androids!
Production is certainly not mass-scale, but enough for prototypes.
All machinery exactly matches the style of the rest of the hall!
And while it's big for a room, it's not so big for a factory!
So, whoever designed this crammed every single space.
Just staring at all this gives you a headache.
It's like mixing Escher architecture with Renaissance and a touch of Giger madness!

0: You couldn't even pick a job! Bye bye money!
At least you got a job, now you got a debt too!
2: You tried, but not that hard.
Title: Sellsword with no sword to sell.
3: It was a nice try, but not enough!

end: The CEO is here. He watches you, inquisitive and annoyed.
You explain your situation, but he cuts you off and says:
"I understand. I'll give you the refund. I just need you to sign an agreement stating you won't do business with us ever again."
"And, of course, not a word about this matter. You can keep the gear you found today. Take it as a sign of goodwill."
You take a moment to read it, and it doesn't look like a scam. Besides, the debt would be too much for you anyway, so you don't have much of a choice.
Also, the gear you found is glorious.
You sign.
It seems all too easy but, hey, defeating a mech must have left an impression!
You get the feeling he was just eager to make you go away, but a win is a win.
Bewildered, now you understand: Weliknweb is building its own version of Skynet!
This is pure madness!
You grab what looks like a golden chip from the center of the room. While you wonder what to do next, a man approaches, followed by four dark figures.
You recognize him instantly: he is the CEO of MEGACORP. You recognize the others too—they are Cyber Samurai.
They don't closely mimic the ancient warriors; their armor is sleek, their full helmets feature a glowing visor, and, of course, they wield nano-katanas.
Three are red and one is black. The former are elite units, while the latter is their captain.
They mean big trouble. But having survived The Countess, you feel a weird surge of adrenaline.
In a cold, measured, and imposing voice, the CEO says: "Surrender, and maybe you..."
Blinded by adrenaline, you dash forward and tackle the CEO while he is still speaking.
You sprint away, with the Samurai hot on your heels.
They try to strike, but you are simply too fast in your armor.
You break through the entrance and head straight for the nearest police station.
The CEO is unconscious but otherwise unharmed.
Things don't go smoothly at first. You face charges of all types, from kidnapping to assault and battery.
But luck is on your side: an attorney, named, with great imagination, "The Shark," has an old score to settle with MEGACORP and takes your case.
He manages to have the golden chip analyzed, and the data on it proved decisive.
The whole ordeal turns into a massive media sensation, which plays heavily in your favor.
Soon, the CEO is indicted, and MEGACORP's shares plummet.
The CEO vanishes. Rumor has it he took refuge in Belize.
MEGACORP is swiftly taken over by a competitor.
Word spreads about you surviving The Countess, and you become a local hero.

0: At least you had a job, too bad you got fired!
1: Your problem solving skills need to be honed.
2: You have some talent, develop it, maybe...!
3: You have talent, but reality is harsh!
4: You got the skills, maybe not the luck!

end: The CEO is here. He watches you, inquisitive and annoyed at first, then surprised when he recognizes you.
You angrily explain your situation and he tries to cut you off but you raise your voice.
He listens to you, his gaze so cold it could freeze the sun.
When you finish, he takes a deep breath and says:
"Fine. We'll give you your back payment. And two years of wages. Take it as a sign of goodwill."
"You just have to sign that NDA and respect it."
You take a moment to read it, and it doesn't look like a scam.
You sign the contract and give it back to him.
With composure, he says: "Get out of my sight and never come back."
You go toward the door and, just before leaving, with a grin, you say: "A pleasure doing business with you."
His stare could pierce a black hole, but he remains silent.
You get the feeling he was just eager to make you go away, but you got what you wanted!
And now you are free to start again!
Bewildered, now you understand: Weliknweb is building its own version of Skynet!
This is pure madness!
Quickly, you connect to the assembly line with your laptop and discover a fully working bot you hadn't noticed before.
It lacks its outer coating but is fully functional; its shape resembles a metallic skeleton.
And you can activate it! On your screen, you read:
"The Count, elite bot. Power level estimated: over 9000. Highly destructive, command with care."
While you are hacking its command protocols, a man approaches, followed by four dark figures.
You recognize him instantly: he is the CEO of MEGACORP. You recognize the others too, they are Cyber Samurai.
They don't closely mimic the ancient warriors; their armor is sleek, their full helmets feature a glowing visor, and, of course, they wield nano-katanas.
Three are red and one is black. The former are elite units, while the latter is their captain.
They mean big trouble. But having survived The Countess, you feel a weird surge of adrenaline.
In a cold, measured, and imposing voice, the CEO says: "Surrender, and maybe you'll die without suffering."
But you aren't just waiting around; you start recording everything with your laptop while instructing The Count.
You command it to protect you, displaying its force without killing anyone.
At a nod from the CEO, the Cyber Samurai launch their assault.
The Count steps into the open and shields you, grabbing a katana and shattering it with its bare grip.
The CEO's face turns pale, and he runs away!
The battle between The Count and the Cyber Samurai is fierce, but the machine quickly displays its absolute superiority.
This leaves you free to pursue the CEO.
You catch up to him and he snarls: "You will regre-"
But before he can finish, you LART him for good! Now he's completely powerless.
You check on The Count; it has successfully disabled all the Samurai. The room is totally unscathed, and you marvel at the mastery required to avoid any damage.
With The Count's aid, you drag the CEO straight to the police station.
The situation is surreal, but upon seeing The Count, not to mention your recording, the officers believe your story.
The whole ordeal turns into a massive media sensation, which plays heavily in your favor.
Soon, the CEO is indicted, and MEGACORP's shares plummet rapidly.
The CEO vanishes. Rumor has it he took refuge in Belize.
MEGACORP is swiftly taken over by a competitor.
Word spreads about you hacking The Count, and you are flooded with job offers and respect from the tech community.
Now you work half as much as before, earn twice as much, and can afford all the vacations you want!
And, of course, you also got your back pay and two years of wages as compensation for moral damages!
Life is good!
